rationale: The citing paper uses Kishore et al. (2013) in its core literature positioning on sales-force incentives, not just as a passing catalog reference. It also cites that paper to support a theory-relevant claim about stronger incentive effects for higher-ability salespeople and to justify using a before-after field intervention design seen in related marketing work. These roles show the citation informs the paper's framing and empirical setup, even though the focal treatment shifts from bonus-vs-commission to activity-based incentives.
relation_description: Rao et al. treat Kishore et al. (2013) as prior empirical and methodological groundwork in field research on sales compensation plans. They build from that output-incentive evidence to motivate and position their ABI intervention as the next step in this research stream.
